CHANGELOG.md:51:* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
--
CHANGELOG.md:54:* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
--
CHANGELOG.md:56:* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
--
CHANGELOG.md:69:* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324))
--
CHANGELOG.md:98:* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
--
CHANGELOG.md:133:* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
--
CHANGELOG.md:138:* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
--
CHANGELOG.md:142:* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
--
CHANGELOG.md:291:When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
--
CHANGELOG.md:328:When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
--
CHANGELOG.md:373:  * Add new experimental rule `multiline-expression-wrapping`. This forces a multiline expression as value in an assignment to start on a separate line. ([#1217](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1217))
--
CHANGELOG.md:375:  * Add new experimental rule `if-else-wrapping`. This enforces that a single line if-statement is kept simple. A single line if-statement may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches a single line if-statement may not be wrapped in a block. ([#812](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/812))
CHANGELOG.md:376:* Wrap the type or value of a function or class parameter in case the maximum line length is exceeded `parameter-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
CHANGELOG.md:377:* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
--
CHANGELOG.md:389:* Add new experimental rule `enum-wrapping` for all code styles. An enum should either be a single line, or each enum entry should be defined on a separate line. ([#1903](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1903))
--
CHANGELOG.md:400:* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`.
--
CHANGELOG.md:408:* Wrap annotations on type arguments in same way as with other constructs `annotation`, `wrapping` ([#1725](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1725))
--
CHANGELOG.md:412:* Fix false positive when a single line statement containing a block having exactly the maximum line length is preceded by a blank line `wrapping` ([#1808](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1808))
--
CHANGELOG.md:418:* Wrap each type parameter in a multiline type parameter list `wrapping` ([#1867](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1867))
--
CHANGELOG.md:422:* Wrap annotated function parameters to a separate line in code style `ktlint_official` only. `function-signature`, `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1908](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1908))
--
CHANGELOG.md:430:* Wrap the parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list (only in `ktlint_official` code style) `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1681](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1681)).
--
CHANGELOG.md:440:* Convert a single line block comment to an EOL comment if not preceded or followed by another code element on the same line `comment-wrapping` ([#1941](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1941))
CHANGELOG.md:441:* Ignore a block comment inside a single line block `comment-wrapping` ([#1942](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1942))
--
CHANGELOG.md:603:* Wrap blocks in case the max line length is exceeded or in case the block contains a new line `wrapping` ([#1643](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1643))
--
CHANGELOG.md:605:* Add basic formatting for context receiver in `indent` rule and new experimental rule `context-receiver-wrapping` ([#1672](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1672))
--
CHANGELOG.md:821:* Prevent false positive in parameter list for which the last value parameter is a destructuring declaration followed by a trailing comma `wrapping` ([#1578](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1578))
--

394→/**
395→ * Creates a sequences of leaves including the [ASTNode] in case it is a [LeafElement] itself. By default, the leaves are traversed in
396→ * forward order. Setting [forward] to `false` changes this to traversal in backward direction.
397→ */
398→public fun ASTNode.leavesIncludingSelf(forward: Boolean = true): Sequence<ASTNode> {
399→    val sequence =
400→        if (isLeaf()) {
401→            sequenceOf(this)
402→        } else {
403→            emptySequence()
404→        }
405→    return sequence.plus(leaves(forward))
406→}
407→
408→/**
409→ * Get all leaves on the same line as the given node including the whitespace indentation. Note that the whitespace indentation may start
410→ * with zero or more newline characters.
411→ */
412→public fun ASTNode.leavesOnLine(): Sequence<ASTNode> {
413→    val lastLeafOnLineOrNull = getLastLeafOnLineOrNull()
414→    return getFirstLeafOnLineOrSelf()
415→        .leavesIncludingSelf()
416→        .takeWhile { lastLeafOnLineOrNull == null || it.prevLeaf() != lastLeafOnLineOrNull }
417→}
418→
419→internal fun ASTNode.getFirstLeafOnLineOrSelf() = prevLeaf { it.textContains('\n') || it.prevLeaf() == null }!!
420→
421→internal fun ASTNode.getLastLeafOnLineOrNull() = nextLeaf { it.textContains('\n') }?.prevLeaf()
422→
423→/**
424→ * Get the total length of all leaves on the same line as the given node including the whitespace indentation but excluding all leading
425→ * newline characters in the whitespace indentation.
426→ */
427→public fun ASTNode.lineLengthWithoutNewlinePrefix(): Int =
428→    leavesOnLine()
429→        .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
430→        // If a line is preceded by a blank line then the ident contains multiple newline chars
431→        .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
432→        // In case the last element on the line would contain a newline then only include chars before that newline. Note that this should
433→        // not occur if the AST is parsed correctly
434→        .takeWhile { it != '\n' }
435→        .length
